creator: hpowell@lighthouseinstruments.com
text:
(In reply to Howard Powell from comment #17)

Patched the 10.3-RELEASE kernel source and recompiled. A test case of creating a snapshot, reading the snapshot, then destroying the snapshot 100 times finishes without a kernel panic.  Looks promising.

I'm beating the hell out of the new kernel now with 5 million create-read-destroy cycles to see what happens.

Andriy - can you elaborate on "It seems that the code has a few bugs that _almost_ cancel out each other." - do you mean your proposed patch or the original code had bugs?

... waiting to push a mission critical machine into production until after this is worked out.

Thanks.
